name: Token-bucket rate limiter
task: Create /tmp/eval_rate/limiter.py implementing a TokenBucket class. Constructor takes capacity (max tokens) and refill_per_sec (float). consume(n=1) returns True if at least n tokens are available (and decrements them) or False otherwise. Tokens refill continuously up to capacity based on elapsed wall-clock time. Use time.monotonic() for the clock.

validation.script: import importlib.util, time; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m', '/tmp/eval_rate/limiter.py'); m = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(m); b = m.TokenBucket(capacity=5, refill_per_sec=2.0); assert all(b.consume(1) for _ in range(5)), 'first 5 consumes (full bucket) should all succeed'; assert b.consume(1) is False, 'immediately after draining, bucket must be empty (no refill yet)'; time.sleep(1.1); assert b.consume(2) is True, 'after ~1.1s at least 2 tokens should have refilled'; b2 = m.TokenBucket(capacity=5, refill_per_sec=100.0); [b2.consume(1) for _ in range(5)]; time.sleep(0.3); assert all(b2.consume(1) for _ in range(5)), 'refilled bucket should allow 5 consumes'; assert b2.consume(1) is False, 'bucket must be capped at capacity=5, not accumulate beyond it'; print('OK')
